answer:
We are given that \( \lim_{n \to \infty} a_n \sum_{i=1}^n a_i^2 = 1 \).  
If \( a_n \) behaves like \( n^{-1/3} \), then \( a_n^2 \sim n^{-2/3} \), and the sum \( \sum_{i=1}^n a_i^2 \sim 3 n^{1/3} \), so the product tends to 3, not 1.  
Adjusting the constant, if \( a_n \sim c n^{-1/3} \), then the sum \( \sum_{i=1}^n a_i^2 \sim 3c^2 n^{1/3} \), and the product tends to \( 3c^3 = 1 \), so \( c = 3^{-1/3} \).
